title: sisymbrium thalianum var. pusillum
intro: Sisymbrium thalianum var. pusillum, also known as Lesser Hairy Mustard or Small-Flowered Hairy Mustard, is a small annual herb belonging to the family Brassicaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Sisymbrium thalianum var. pusillum include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to aid in digestion and relieve rheumatic pain. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
